title: Assisted Deputyship Application Programme (ADAP)
text:
Assisted Deputyship Application Programme (ADAP) - SupportGoWhere
Assisted Deputyship Application Programme (ADAP)
Share link
ADAP supports caregivers of children without mental capacity to apply to be court-appointed deputies, through the simplified deputyship process. As a deputy, the caregiver can make legal decisions for the child when he/she turns 21.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Guidance provided for simplified deputyship applications for caregivers of persons with disabilities and without mental capacity
Acceptance of mental capacity assessment reports from SPED Schools, DACs, SWs, or the MSF-appointed ADAP Agency
Apply now
Collapse all sections
About this support

Programme provides guidance/facilitation, for caregivers of persons with no mental capacity to manage their own matters in adulthood, to apply to Family Justice Courts to be appointed as legal deputies for their child. This is a simplified process that is also more affordable, compared to application for deputyship under standard route.

Courts may accept mental capacity assessment reports prepared by psychologists from Special Education Schools (SPED Schools), selected Day Activity Centres (DACs), Sheltered Workshops (SWs), or the MSF-appointed ADAP Agency in lieu of a medical report. This applies only when the child is enrolled in a SPED school, DAC, or SW that is onboard the ADAP programme. In all other cases, the mental capacity assessment must be conducted by a medical capacity assessment must be conducted by a medical doctor or psychiatrist. 

Who is this for?

Your child is:

between 18 and 21 years old as well as in the graduating cohort in a Special Education (SPED) school; or

above 18 years old and enrolled in an MSF-funded Day Activity Centre (DAC) or Sheltered Workshop (SW).

You agree to use the standard list of powers/orders available under the Integrated Family Application Management System (iFAMS) when filing a simplified Deputyship application.

All family members and any other caregivers agree for you to make the application (i.e. it is an uncontested case).

How to apply?

Caregivers can approach the relevant staff in the SPED school, Day Activity Centre or Sheltered Workshop to indicate interest to apply for Deputyship. The school or centre will work with an MSF-appointed ADAP Agency to guide the parent/caregiver in their Deputyship application.



For Caregivers whose child with disability is not enrolled in a service, please contact ADAP Agency directly via phone or email for enquiries on deputyship application.

Where can I find help?

View more details

ADAP Agency Hotline: 6547 8503

Submit an enquiry

Referral is required for application.

MINDS
